edibility: Choice edible; cap commonly breaded and fried like a schnitzel. Stem usually too fibrous to eat, though the ring can be removed for cooking.
taste: Nutty, mild, slightly sweet flavor. Firm meaty cap with pleasant texture when properly prepared.
search: Found late summer through autumn in pastures and woodland clearings. ID features include a distinctive sliding ring on the stem, snakeskin pattern on the cap, and large size (can reach 40cm). DANGEROUS look-alikes include Chlorophyllum molybdites with green spores, which causes severe gastrointestinal poisoning.

toxicity.symptoms: N/A — not toxic if correctly identified; warning: lookalikes may cause severe GI distress
toxicity.antidote: N/A — not toxic if correctly identified
